While an[outfit](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/fortnite-creative/fortnite-creative-glossary#outfit)is what you use to change a character's appearance, a wrap is what you use to change the appearance of a gun or vehicle.

 X-axis 
See[axis](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/fortnite-creative/fortnite-creative-glossary#axis).

 XL island 
An extra-large or XL island uses memory in a different way from other islands. Smaller islands work by loading everything into memory and keeping it there all the time. The memory bar at the top of the screen reports the amount of memory used and available. For larger islands, unused and unseen props and landscape features are not kept in memory. The memory bar is still in view, but you can also use a heat map to see a color-coded overlay that shows which parts of the island are more loaded.

 XP 
Short for experience points, these are points earned when playing Fortnite games that are awarded for completing quests and other accomplishments in a game.

 XR 
XR refers to real-and virtual-combined environments and human-machine interactions made by computer technology. It includes technologies like virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), mixed reality (MR), and everything in between. Rumor has it that the X was chosen because the top half represented the V in Virtual Reality, and the bottom half represented the A in Augmented Reality.

 Y-axis 
See[axis](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/fortnite-creative/fortnite-creative-glossary#axis).

 yaw 
See[orientation](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/fortnite-creative/fortnite-creative-glossary#orientation).

 Z-axis 
See **[axis](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/fortnite/fortnite-creative-glossary#axis)**.

 z-score 
In statistical analysis, a z-score is a way to measure how far from the **mean**(average) any data value is based on a standardized scale. Z-scores identify **outliers**in a dataset by finding those values (the outliers) that are unusually distant from the mean.

 Zipline 
A device that you can use to create fast traversal on your island, with twists and turns that are unique and fun.

 zombie horde 
A game mode where individuals or teams defend against hordes of zombies and eliminate as many as possible.

 zone war 
An elimination game mode with the objective to be last person standing in the center of a storm.

*   [ecosystem](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/search?query=ecosystem)

* * *

Ask questions and help your peers[Developer Forums](https://forums.unrealengine.com/categories?tag=fortnite)

Write your own tutorials or read those from others[Learning Library](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/fortnite/learning)

title: Game jargon - Fortnite | British Esports Federation
content:
## Hot drop:

A location or point of interest on the map which is a popular place to land after leaving the battle bus. These places will normally have some good loot available, or will just be a really good spot on the map.

## Meds:

Refers to any item that can increase your health.

## Minis:

Refers to the smallest shield available in the game that grants you 25 shields at a time.

## Padding:

Refers to when someone uses a launchpad.

## Point of interest / POI:

Any place on the map that has a name becomes a point of interest – e.g – Tilted Towers.

## Reboot card / van: [...] ## Battle bus:

The flying vehicle that brings the players to the island at the start of the match. Players jump out of the bus to parachute to their location of choice on the map to start the game.

## Bandies:

Colloquial way of saying bandages. These can be used to heal a player 15hp every 5 seconds, up to 75hp.

## Big pot:

Refers to the Rare Shield Potion that grants players an additional 50 shields when consumed.

## Camper / Bush camper:

Someone who hides in a bush during the match and waits until there’s an opportunity to get a kill to come out. If you are in a bush, nobody can see you, but you can see them.

## Cracked: [...] ## Cracked:

Can have a few different meanings, including:  
– To describe a player that is good at the game  
– To describe the noise and visual cue of an enemy shield breaking

## Downed:

In a team-based mode, players will be knocked down before getting eliminated – giving the opportunity for their teammates to revive them. Players will use this callout to signal they are in need of a revive, or if the enemy has a player downed. If a player is downed, you can eliminate them by doing more damage to them.

## Dub:

Another way of saying you won a game.

## Heal-off:

When the storm has fully closed in, players are required to use as many healing abilities as possible to stay alive. The person who has the most, or can find the most, wins.

## Hot drop:

Game jargon – Fortnite

![](https://britishesports.org/wp-content/uploads/fly-images/20490/FORTNITE-1200X800-767x710.jpg)

# Game jargon – Fortnite

![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/80a9952dcc8f96e64fa39611dde1ba9330a4bd3c52532e8b7157eda0a12da47c?s=50&d=mm&r=g)

Have you ever been in a game of Fortnite and heard some terminology you don’t know the meaning of?  
  
In this guide, we share some of the most common terms or phrases used in Fortnite, and what they mean.

## Battle bus:

The flying vehicle that brings the players to the island at the start of the match. Players jump out of the bus to parachute to their location of choice on the map to start the game.

## Bandies:

Colloquial way of saying bandages. These can be used to heal a player 15hp every 5 seconds, up to 75hp.

## Big pot:

Refers to the Rare Shield Potion that grants players an additional 50 shields when consumed.

## Camper / Bush camper:

Someone who hides in a bush during the match and waits until there’s an opportunity to get a kill to come out. If you are in a bush, nobody can see you, but you can see them.

## Cracked:

Can have a few different meanings, including:  
– To describe a player that is good at the game  
– To describe the noise and visual cue of an enemy shield breaking

## Downed:

In a team-based mode, players will be knocked down before getting eliminated – giving the opportunity for their teammates to revive them. Players will use this callout to signal they are in need of a revive, or if the enemy has a player downed. If a player is downed, you can eliminate them by doing more damage to them.

## Dub:

Another way of saying you won a game.

## Heal-off:

When the storm has fully closed in, players are required to use as many healing abilities as possible to stay alive. The person who has the most, or can find the most, wins.

## Hot drop:

A location or point of interest on the map which is a popular place to land after leaving the battle bus. These places will normally have some good loot available, or will just be a really good spot on the map.

## Meds:

Refers to any item that can increase your health.

## Minis:

Refers to the smallest shield available in the game that grants you 25 shields at a time.

## Padding:

Refers to when someone uses a launchpad.

## Point of interest / POI:

Any place on the map that has a name becomes a point of interest – e.g – Tilted Towers.

## Reboot card / van:

When a player dies in a team-based mode, they drop a reboot card, and a teammate has 90-seconds to pick the card up before it expires. When the player has the card, they need to take it to a reboot van to revive their teammate.

## Siphon:

A mechanic that allows players to gain health and shield when they eliminate an enemy.

## Spawn island:

The initial place where everyone spawns before going on the battle bus. It is possible to get back to this island during a match, but it is most likely to be deserted.

## Surge:

If there are too many players alive at certain points in the game, a storm surge will occur to eliminate those who have done the least damage in the match. The surge finishes when there is a certain number of players remaining in the match.

## The Storm:

The large ring that surrounds the map at the beginning of the game, and shrinks as the game progresses. If you are caught in the storm, you take damage, so players must stay safe in the storm eye.

## Thirst:

Refers to when a player rushes to kill a player after they have downed them.

## Zone:

Refers to the area in the centre that will not be closed in by the storm until the next phase. This zone is safe until the storm eye closes in.

These are just some of the many things you may hear in a game of Fortnite, but there are many more things to learn as you go through your matches.

You can also check out some of our other Game Jargon guides for [Overwatch](https://britishesports.org/general-esports-info/game-jargon-overwatch/), [Valorant](https://britishesports.org/general-esports-info/game-jargon-valorant/), [League of Legends](https://britishesports.org/general-esports-info/game-jargon-league-of-legends/), [CS:GO](https://britishesports.org/general-esports-info/game-jargon-csgo/), and [Rocket League](https://britishesports.org/general-esports-info/game-jargon-rocket-league/) on our website.

Want us to do a specific guide for a game? Let us know on the [British Esports Twitter](https://twitter.com/british_esports?lang=en), and we may use your idea in the future.

| Platform | Number of Players (millions) |

| Console | 78 |

These figures highlight the game's widespread popularity and success across different gaming platforms, indicating the strong presence of Fortnite in the gaming industry.

### Implications on Game Development

The diverse player base across different platforms has significant implications for Fortnite's development strategy. As different platforms have distinct capabilities and controls, the game's mechanics and interface must be tailored to the specific requirements of each platform. [...] These statistics demonstrate the high level of player engagement in Fortnite and indicate that a significant portion of players dedicate substantial time to the game each week.

### Most Popular Game Modes

The Fortnite community has diverse tastes when it comes to game modes, but specific preferences emerge from the data. According to Fortnite Tracker, Team Rumble was the most popular game mode in Season 8, accounting for over 20% of all games played .

| Game Mode | Percentage of Games Played |
 --- |
| Team Rumble | 20% |
| Squads | 60% |
| Duos | 19% |
| Solo | 20% |
| Zero Build | 1.4% | [...] ## Player Engagement in Fortnite

Examining the player engagement in Fortnite provides valuable insights into the game's popularity and the preferences of its player base. This involves looking at both the average playtime statistics and the popularity of different game modes.

### Average Playtime Statistics

On average, Fortnite players recorded a playtime of 21 hours per week in October 2023. The breakdown reveals that 17% of players spent more than 35 hours weekly playing the game, while 30% played for over 20 hours per week .

| Player Percentage | Average Playtime (Hours/Week) |
 --- |
| 17% | over 35 |
| 30% | over 20 |
| Remaining | Less than 20 |

# Fortnite Statistics: From Victory Royale to Stats Galore

Dive into Fortnite statistics: from victory royale to player engagement, discover the game's success story.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68802957398fd97b576bbaf9/6890fc2a8741b3e1b073742b_6885fd91523f0b94ba456e0a_64e25fa4e073f39027d914a6_steven-transparent.avif)

Steven Zauderer

February 19, 2026

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6880a5185fab16193d5d925d/68909ea94554dbebc3868648_65e137517a76916f4964c271_fortnite%2520statistics.avif)

## The Top 10 Key Statistics About Fortnite

* Fortnite has made an estimated $9.1 billion since its launch in 2017, with revenue primarily coming from in-game purchases.
* In 2020 alone, Fortnite amassed over $1.8 billion in earnings.
* As of May 2020, Fortnite had over 350 million registered players, with about 2.2 billion hours spent in-game each month.
* Fortnite is playable on multiple platforms, including consoles, PCs, and mobile devices, making it accessible to a wide variety of players.
* The free battle royale mode, which allows up to 100 players to fight until only one player (or team) is left standing, has played a significant role in Fortnite's success.
* Fortnite's financial success is undeniable, despite being a free-to-play game, thanks to its in-game purchases.
* Epic Games, the company behind Fortnite, was reported to be making approximately $300 million a month in revenue as of late.
* The Fortnite World Cup in 2019 had a staggering $30 million prize pool, further solidifying the game's position as a cultural phenomenon.
* Fortnite has been successful in transforming the gaming landscape by integrating collaborations with artists and creating virtual experiences within the game.
* Fortnite's focus on collaboration and inclusivity has created a cooperative and engaging environment that enhances the overall gaming experience.

## Unraveling Fortnite's Success

Fortnite, a multi-platform game developed by Epic Games, has been a roaring success since its release. The game's success can be seen in its revenue figures, growth, and the multitude of factors that have contributed to its popularity.

### Revenue Figures and Growth

According to [**Wired**](https://www.wired.co.uk/article/fortnite-success-business-model-season-five), as of July 2021, Fortnite was estimated to have made $9.1 billion since its launch. This is an impressive figure, especially considering that the game is free to play. The revenue primarily comes from in-game purchases, where players can buy virtual currency (known as V-bucks) to obtain cosmetic items like skins, emotes, and battle passes.

Fortnite consistently ranks as one of the top-grossing games according to [**Gamerant**](https://gamerant.com/fortnite-best-creative-play-friends/). In 2020 alone, Fortnite amassed over $1.8 billion in earnings. These figures illustrate Fortnite's financial success and its profitability as a game.

| Year | Revenue |
| --- | --- |
| 2020 | $1.8 billion |
| Total (up to July 2021) | $9.1 billion |

### Factors Contributing to Success

Several factors have contributed to Fortnite's success. One of the main reasons behind its popularity is the free battle royale mode, which allows up to 100 players to fight until only one player (or team) is left standing. This mode offers a thrilling and competitive gaming experience that has appealed to millions of players worldwide. [**Wired**](https://www.wired.co.uk/article/fortnite-success-business-model-season-five) notes that this mode has played a significant role in Fortnite's success.

Another factor is Fortnite's expansive reach. It had over 350 million registered players as of May 2020, with about 2.2 billion hours spent in-game each month according to [**Business of Apps**](https://www.businessofapps.com/data/fortnite-statistics/). The game is playable on multiple platforms, including consoles, PCs, and mobile devices, making it accessible to a wide variety of players.

Moreover, the success of Fortnite has enabled Epic Games to expand its offerings beyond gaming. This includes collaborations with artists, movie nights, and virtual experiences within the game, which have made Fortnite a platform for entertainment and social interaction rather than just a game.

Unraveling the key Fortnite statistics helps us understand how this game has managed to dominate the gaming industry. From impressive revenue figures to an ever-growing player base, Fortnite's success shows no signs of slowing down.

## Fortnite's Free Battle Royale Mode

One of the chief reasons behind Fortnite's phenomenal success is its free Battle Royale mode. This section delves into the popularity and accessibility of the mode and its impact on Fortnite's revenue.

### Popularity and Accessibility

Fortnite's free Battle Royale mode has significantly contributed to its popularity and success, reaching a staggering number of over 350 million registered players as of May 2020. This mode, which pits players against one another in an ever-shrinking map until only one player or team remains, has proven to be a hit among gamers worldwide.

The game's accessibility is another significant factor in its popularity. Available on multiple platforms, including PC, consoles, and mobile devices, Fortnite has managed to reach an expansive audience. The free-to-play model, combined with the option of in-game purchases, has allowed the game to appeal to both casual gamers and dedicated fans.

Moreover, Fortnite has kept players engaged by continuously evolving. The game has seen the addition and removal of various elements such as guns, items, map locations, game modes, characters, and vehicles that keep the gameplay fresh and exciting [2].

### Implications on Revenue

Despite being a free-to-play game, Fortnite's financial success is undeniable. The game consistently ranks as one of the top-grossing games, primarily due to its in-game purchases. In 2020 alone, Fortnite amassed over $1.8 billion in earnings, illustrating its profitability [3].

> Epic Games, the company behind Fortnite, was reported to be making approximately $300 million a month in revenue as of late [2]. This revenue comes from a variety of sources, including the sale of virtual currency, cosmetic items, and battle passes.

These Fortnite statistics underscore the game's mastery of the free-to-play business model. By offering a compelling, accessible game at no cost and monetizing through optional purchases, Fortnite has found a way to generate significant revenue while keeping players engaged and entertained.

## The Impact of Fortnite World Cup

One of the pivotal events in the history of Fortnite is undoubtedly the Fortnite World Cup. This event not only helped the game gain global recognition but also significantly influenced the player base.

### Prize Pool and Global Recognition

The Fortnite World Cup in 2019 had a staggering $30 million prize pool, further solidifying the game's position as a cultural phenomenon. This event highlighted the competitive aspect of the game, showcasing the skills of some of the best players from around the world. The magnitude of the prize pool also attracted significant international attention, bringing Fortnite into the spotlight.

The massive prize pool of the Fortnite World Cup underscores the scale of the eSports scene surrounding Fortnite. This event has played a crucial role in cementing the game's reputation not only as an entertaining pastime but also as a viable competitive platform with substantial financial rewards.

### Influence on Player Base

The [**Fortnite World Cup**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fortnite_World_Cup) had a notable impact on the game's player base. The global recognition that the event brought to Fortnite attracted new players, intrigued by the potential of competitive gaming and the prospect of substantial prizes.

Moreover, the World Cup served as an inspiration for existing players, motivating them to hone their skills and compete at the highest level. This has led to an increase in the overall activity and engagement within the game, contributing to the game's sustained popularity.

In conclusion, the Fortnite World Cup has had a profound impact on the game's trajectory. From enhancing its global reputation to expanding its player base, this event has played a significant role in Fortnite's success story. As the game continues to evolve, the influence of such large-scale events will undoubtedly continue to shape the Fortnite landscape.

## Fortnite's Expanding Horizons

The success of Fortnite, as captured in the astounding Fortnite statistics, has allowed the game's developer, Epic Games, to innovate and expand beyond the traditional boundaries of the gaming industry.

### Collaborations and Virtual Experiences

Fortnite has been successful in transforming the gaming landscape by integrating collaborations with artists and creating virtual experiences within the game. This includes in-game concerts and movie nights which have added a new dimension to the gaming experience, making it more immersive and interactive for the players.

Moreover, the game has constantly evolved over the years, adding and removing different elements such as guns, items, map locations, game modes, characters, and vehicles. This constant innovation keeps the game fresh and exciting for the players, thereby contributing to its popularity [2].

Another key aspect of Fortnite's success is its focus on collaboration. Game modes like doubles, teams, and 50 v 50 encourage players to work together by sharing resources, reviving fallen teammates, and displaying altruistic behavior. This creates a cooperative and engaging environment that enhances the overall gaming experience [2].

### Expansion Beyond Gaming Industry

The impact of Fortnite extends far beyond the gaming industry. With a staggering number of over 40 million active monthly players as of January, and a total of 125 million players and counting, Fortnite has established a strong presence in popular culture.

> Fortnite's approach to character assignment is also worth noting. The game randomizes character assignment to promote inclusivity and encourage players to experience different perspectives. This fosters a cooperative atmosphere, making the game more appealing to a diverse range of players [2].

In conclusion, Fortnite's expansion beyond the gaming industry and its continuous innovation through collaborations and virtual experiences have played a significant role in its success. The game's ability to adapt and evolve while fostering a collaborative and inclusive environment has made it a standout in the gaming world, as evidenced by the impressive Fortnite statistics.

## Player Engagement in Fortnite

Examining the player engagement in Fortnite provides valuable insights into the game's popularity and the preferences of its player base. This involves looking at both the average playtime statistics and the popularity of different game modes.

### Average Playtime Statistics

On average, Fortnite players recorded a playtime of 21 hours per week in October 2023. The breakdown reveals that 17% of players spent more than 35 hours weekly playing the game, while 30% played for over 20 hours per week [5].

| Player Percentage | Average Playtime (Hours/Week) |
| --- | --- |
| 17% | over 35 |
| 30% | over 20 |
| Remaining | Less than 20 |

These statistics demonstrate the high level of player engagement in Fortnite and indicate that a significant portion of players dedicate substantial time to the game each week.

### Most Popular Game Modes

The Fortnite community has diverse tastes when it comes to game modes, but specific preferences emerge from the data. According to Fortnite Tracker, Team Rumble was the most popular game mode in Season 8, accounting for over 20% of all games played [6].

| Game Mode | Percentage of Games Played |
| --- | --- |
| Team Rumble | 20% |
| Squads | 60% |
| Duos | 19% |
| Solo | 20% |
| Zero Build | 1.4% |

In terms of team size, squads were the most favored, with more than 60% of all Fortnite games played in Season 8 being Squads matches. Solo matches accounted for nearly 20% of games, while Duos made up around 19% of the matches during that period.

Interestingly, despite its popularity, Team Rumble also had the longest average game length, lasting an average of 17 minutes and 54 seconds per match in Season 8.

These Fortnite statistics reveal that while players have diverse preferences, certain game modes and team sizes are more popular than others. This information is valuable for understanding player engagement and can help guide future game development and updates.

## Platform-Based Popularity

As a part of our exploration of Fortnite statistics, one of the most interesting is the breakdown of player demographics by platform. This aspect of the game's popularity offers significant insights into the preferences of the game's diverse user base.

### Console vs PC vs Mobile Players

Fortnite's cross-platform availability has played a major role in its success. Boasting a massive player base, the game has been able to reach a wide variety of gamers, regardless of their preferred gaming platform. As per data cited from [**Gamerant**](https://gamerant.com/fortnite-best-creative-play-friends/), Fortnite has over 78 million players on console, 37 million on PC, and a staggering 357 million on mobile devices. This distribution showcases the game's broad appeal and accessibility.

| Platform | Number of Players (millions) |
| --- | --- |
| Console | 78 |
| PC | 37 |
| Mobile | 357 |

These figures highlight the game's widespread popularity and success across different gaming platforms, indicating the strong presence of Fortnite in the gaming industry.

### Implications on Game Development

The diverse player base across different platforms has significant implications for Fortnite's development strategy. As different platforms have distinct capabilities and controls, the game's mechanics and interface must be tailored to the specific requirements of each platform.

For instance, the console and PC versions of Fortnite require more complex controls and can support higher graphical fidelity, whereas the mobile version must be optimized for touchscreen controls and lower-powered hardware. This presents a unique challenge for the game's developers, who must ensure a consistent, high-quality gaming experience across all platforms.

> Moreover, the high number of mobile players underscores how mobile gaming has become a significant segment of the market, influencing the development and marketing strategies of Fortnite and other popular games. This trend is expected to continue, as more gamers turn to mobile devices for their gaming needs.

The platform-based popularity of Fortnite illustrates how the game has successfully navigated the challenges of cross-platform development to cater to a diverse and global gaming audience. It is a testament to the game's adaptability, accessibility, and enduring appeal, further solidifying its stature in the gaming industry.

## Fortnite's Influence on eSports

As one of the most popular games globally, Fortnite has made impactful strides in the eSports industry. The game's competitive dynamics and immense popularity have positioned it as a key player in the realm of eSports.

### Fortnite World Cup and eSports

The Fortnite World Cup, held in 2019, was a testament to the game's monumental influence on eSports. The tournament boasted an impressive $30 million prize pool, one of the largest in eSports history [4]. This major event drew global attention, further solidifying Fortnite's position as a cultural phenomenon and a force to be reckoned with in the competitive gaming space.

| Event | Year | Prize Pool |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fortnite World Cup | 2019 | $30 million |

This not only highlights the competitive aspect of the game but also emphasizes the scale of the eSports scene surrounding Fortnite. The immense prize pool and global recognition have positioned Fortnite as a staple in the eSports industry.

### The Role of Competitive Gaming

Fortnite's influence in eSports extends beyond major tournaments like the World Cup. The game's competitive nature and unique battle royale mode have made it an attractive platform for competitive gaming. With regular updates and new features, Fortnite has managed to keep its gameplay fresh and engaging, fostering a dynamic competitive environment.

In the world of eSports, Fortnite has set new standards, especially in terms of accessibility. The game's free-to-play model has made it accessible to a wide range of players, further contributing to its success in the eSports industry.

Fortnite's influence on eSports is a significant part of the game's overall success story. The game's popularity, combined with its competitive nature and massive prize pools, has played a crucial role in shaping the landscape of competitive gaming and eSports. As Fortnite continues to evolve and grow, its influence on eSports is expected to continue making waves in the gaming industry.

# Fortnite Statistics 2025: A Comprehensive Overview

Fortnite remains a global gaming phenomenon in 2025, continuing to set records in player engagement, revenue, and cultural impact. This article delves into the latest statistics, player demographics, game modes, and esports performance, offering a detailed snapshot of Fortnite's current landscape.

<https://forum.enscape3d.com/wcf/index.php?user/117593-best-ai-porn-generator/>

<https://forum.figma.com/chat-and-connect-27/candy-ai-reviews-pricing-alternatives-free-trial-in-2025-no-filter-42771>

  

## Player Base and Activity

## Registered and Active Users

* Registered Players: Over 650 million registered users globally as of 2025, up from 350 million in 2020
* Daily Active Users: Fortnite maintains an average of 30–40 million daily active users throughout 2025, with some sources reporting up to 60 million during peak periods
* Concurrent Players: Average concurrent users range from 1.15 to 3.5 million at any given time, with record peaks during major events

## Peak Events

* Record Concurrent Players: The "Remix: The Finale" event in November 2024 drew over 14.3 million concurrent players
* Highest Daily Player Count: The OG 2023 season saw 44.7 million players in a single day[2](https://beebom.com/fortnite-player-count/).

* Player Loyalty: 37% of Fortnite players do not play any other games, highlighting the game's unique appeal[8](https://www.esports.net/news/fortnite/fortnite-player-count/).

## Game Modes and Preferences

Fortnite offers a variety of modes, each with distinct player bases and gameplay styles:

## Player Engagement

* Average Playtime: Players spend 8–10 hours per week on Fortnite[2](https://beebom.com/fortnite-player-count/).
* Website Traffic: Fortnite's website receives 17.5 million monthly visits[1](https://explodingtopics.com/blog/fortnite-stats).
* Streaming Popularity: Over 13 million hours of Fortnite are streamed weekly on Twitch, with peak esports viewership reaching over 2.3 million

## Revenue and Spending

* Historical Revenue: Fortnite generated over $9 billion in its first two years[1](https://explodingtopics.com/blog/fortnite-stats).
* Annual Revenue: Despite fluctuations, revenue has rebounded since 2018 and continues to climb[4](https://activeplayer.io/fortnite/).
* In-Game Spending: The average player spends over $100 on in-game purchases[9](https://techjury.net/blog/fortnite-statistics/).
* Top Spender Country: The United States accounts for 63% of total player spending[4](https://activeplayer.io/fortnite/).

## Esports and Competitive Scene

* Esports Tournaments: Major events like the FNCS Pro-Am continue to draw large audiences, with over 600,000 peak viewers in 2025
* Collaborations: Frequent collaborations with celebrities, brands, and franchises (e.g., Marvel, DC, Star Wars) boost player counts and engagement during special events[7](https://dotesports.com/fortnite/news/how-many-players-does-fortnite-have).

## 

Fortnite's sustained popularity in 2025 is driven by its evolving content, diverse game modes, and robust community engagement. The game remains a leader in both casual and competitive gaming, with impressive statistics across all metrics.

## 

All data and statistics are drawn from recent industry reports and analytics platforms

# Fortnite Statistics 2026 (Player Count & Revenue)

Fortnite has over 650 million registered players worldwide. The U.S. alone accounts for 108.2 million players (21.1%), while Russia, Brazil, and Poland contribute significantly to its diverse fan base.

Additionally, Fortnite’s daily active users average 1.3 million, but surge to 44.7 million during live events, proving the game’s unmatched cultural impact.

## **Fortnite Statistic 2026: Top Highlights**

### **Fortnite Monthly Active Users (MAU)**

Currently, Fortnite has 650 million registered users and roughly 1.3 million daily average players.

The highest officially reported MAU peak was 126 million in 2023, following the “Fortnite OG” event season. Fortnite attracted 1 million registered players (by August 2017) within 1 month of its July 2017 launch.

According to SimilarWeb analytics, Fortnite’s website attracted 16.79 million visits as of October 2025. This was 29.92% higher compared to September 2025.

Mobile devices account for 39.56% of this traffic, while desktop accounts for 60.44%.

**Sources**: [SimilarWeb](https://pro.similarweb.com/#/digitalsuite/websiteanalysis/overview/website-performance/*/999/1m?webSource=Total&key=fortnite.com), ActivePlayer.io, Business of Apps

### **Fortnite Daily Active Users (DAU)**

On average, Fortnite attracts around 1.5 million daily active users during regular periods.

However, the platform demonstrates extraordinary growth during special in-game events, with DAUs surging to 44.7 million.

This is nearly 30 times higher than its usual baseline.

**Source:** [Electro IQ](https://electroiq.com/stats/analyzing-gaming-app-popularity-through-roblox-vs-fortnite-statistics/)

### **How Many People Play Fortnite?**

Fortnite’s daily active player count has been decreasing since February 2025, but it regained some audience in November 2025 and has been increasing since then. In December 2025, average daily active users sit around 1,302,591, whereas peak users were around 2.73 million users.

The highest peak users came in November 2025, with 9.75 million players.

However, compared to January’s peak of 2.38 million, the player base has shrunk by 42% over the past nine months, suggesting a possible decline in long-term engagement or market saturation.

**The following table displays the daily active Fortnite users by month:**

| **Month** | **Fortnite Daily Active Players** | **Peak Users** |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Source:** [activeplayer.io](https://activeplayer.io/fortnite/).

### **Fortnite Revenue Statistics**

Fortnite earned over $24.8 billion in total revenue by 2022.

The game generated $5.8 billion in revenue in 2021, which was the highest revenue of all time. After 2022, Fortnite hasn’t disclosed its revenue or income report.

![Fortnite Revenue Statistics](https://www.demandsage.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Fortnite-Revenue-Statistics.png "Fortnite Revenue Statistics")

**The following table displays the revenue generated by Fortnite over the years:**

| **Year** | **Revenue Generated** |
| --- | --- |
| 2018 | $5.4 billion |
| 2019 | $3.7 billion |
| 2020 | $5.1 billion |
| 2021 | $4.8 billion |
| 2022 | $5.8 billion |

**Source:** [**Statista**](https://www.statista.com/statistics/1440682/fortnite-revenue/)

After a five-year legal battle, Fortnite is back on the Apple App Store in the U.S.

In December 2025, Fortnite generated $2.79 million in U.S. mobile app revenue.

Historically, Fortnite’s mobile revenue peaked at $46.09 million in Dec 2018, but by May 2025 (after its App Store comeback), revenue had dropped to a staggering 97.5% decline from its peak:

| **Month & Year** | **Revenue (Million U.S. Dollars)** |
| --- | --- |
| **December 2025** | $2.79 |
| **November 2025** | $2.03 |
| **October 2025** | $1.77 |
| **September 2025** | $1.15 |
| **August 2025** | $1.57 |
| **July 2025** | $1.52 |
| **June 2025** | $2.09 |
| **May 2025** | $0.94 |
| **March 2019** | $25.89 |
| **February 2019** | $28.58 |
| **January 2019** | $26.88 |
| **December 2018** | $46.09 |
| **November 2018** | $26.94 |

**Source:** [Statista](https://www.statista.com/statistics/1616022/fortnite-player-spending-app/)

### **Fortnite Player Count**

**Fortnite has over 650 million registered players.**

Fortnite hit a major milestone in 2023, reaching 650 million registered players, the highest number to date.  The player base grew by 150 million from 2022 (500 million) to 2023 (650 million), showing a substantial surge in engagement.

From 2017 to 2023, Fortnite saw impressive growth, jumping from 20 million to 650 million registered players. However, the highest increase was recorded between 2018 and 2020, when the number of players surged from 125 million to 350 million, marking a period of rapid expansion.

![Fortnite All-Time Player Count](https://www.demandsage.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Fortnite-All-Time-Player-Count.jpeg "Fortnite All-Time Player Count")

**The following table displays the registered users of Fortnite over the years:**

| **Year** | **Fortnite Registered Players** |
| --- | --- |
| 2023 | 650 million |
| 2022 | 500 million |
| 2021 | 400 million |
| 2020 | 350 million |
| 2019 | 250 million |
| 2018 | 125 million |
| 2017 | 20 million |

**Source: [Statista](https://www.statista.com/statistics/746230/fortnite-players/)**

**Source:** Statista

### **Fortnite Streaming Stats**

| 2024 (Till March) | 165.5 million |

### **Fortnite Events**

More than 5 million concurrent viewers were recorded on the Fortnite Doomsday event across Twitch and YouTube in June 2020. Besides, the in-game event accumulated more than 11.8 million hours of viewership.

During Travis Scott’s Fortnite music performance, more than 8.09 million cumulative hours were spent on live streaming by Fortnite users.

Furthermore, during Steve Aoki’s performance, people spent 7.7 million hours live-streaming.

On the other hand, DJ Diplo drew the attention of live viewers for 6.01 million hours.

**The following table displays the Fortnite events streaming hours in the year 2020:**

| **Event** | **Date** | **Total Hours Streamed** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Doomsday 2020 | June 5, 2020. | 11.8 million |
| Travis Scott and Fortnite Present: Astronomical | May 9, 2020. | 8.09 million |
| Fortnite Party Royale Steve Aoki | May 2, 2020. | 7.7 million |
| Diplo Presents Higher Ground | Apr 23, 2020. | 6.01 million |

**Source:** Newzoo

### **Fortnite Global Player Spending Stats**

On the contrary, 21% of the players did not make any purchases on Fortnite and were successful in resisting the temptation.

![Fortnite Global Player Spending Stats](https://www.demandsage.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/fortnite-global-player-spending-stats.png)

**The following table displays the spending habits of Fortnite players:**

| **Money Spent On Fortnite** | **Percentage Of Fortnite Players** |
| --- | --- |
| Players who did not spend money on Fortnite | 77% |
| Players that did not spend money on Fortnite | 21% |
| Did not prefer to say. | 2% |

**Source:** Statista.

| **Year** | **Average In-Game Spending By Fortnite Players** |
| --- | --- |
| 2018 | $84.67 |
| 2020 | $102.42 |

**Source:** Statista.

**The following table displays the categories on which Fortnite players spend the most money:**

| **Category** | **Percentage Of Fortnite Players** |
| --- | --- |
| Outfit and character | 58.9% |
| Gliders | 18.06% |
| Harvesting tools | 13.52% |
| Emotes | 9.52% |

**Source:** Statista

### **Fortnite Security Statistics**

Additionally, another company reported that they were able to detect 53,000 scams on Fortnite. Of those, 86% of the scams were generated from [social media](https://www.demandsage.com/social-media-users/) platforms as per our research, and 11% were from web domains. At the same time, 2% of them were from YouTube.

**Source:** UpGuard, Zero Fox.

### **Fortnite Battle Royale**

68% of gamers worldwide play Battle Royale. 89.8% of the players in China play Battle Royale.

**The following table displays the percentage of Fortnite players from different countries who play Battle Royale:**

| **Country** | **Percentage Of Battle Royale Players** |
| --- | --- |
| China | 89.8% |
| Germany | 53.6% |
| India | 82.8% |
| Indonesia | 74.6% |
| South Korea | 63.2% |
| United Kingdom | 49.4% |
| United States | 46.6% |
| Vietnam | 84.2% |
| Worldwide | 68% |

**Source:** [Exploding Topics](https://explodingtopics.com/).

These numbers reflect how regional gaming trends differ, as highlighted in the latest [gamer statistics](https://www.demandsage.com/gamers-statistics/).

### **Fortnite On Twitch**

Fortnite averaged 42.67K Twitch viewers, as of September 2025, reflecting a stable baseline compared to earlier peaks.

The highest spike for Fortnite Twitch viewership stats came in November 2023 with 164K average viewers, fueled by the OG map re-release.

However, the game experienced one of its lowest engagement points in November 2022, with only 38.7K viewers, before rebounding in 2023.

**Here is a monthly breakdown of Fortnite Twitch viewership between 2018 and 2025:**

| **Month & Year** | **Avg. Viewers (in 1,000s)** |
| --- | --- |
| **Sep 2025** | 42.67 |
| **Aug 2025** | 48.58 |
| **July 2025** | 44.97 |
| **Jun 2025** | 49.27 |
| **May 2025** | 48.35 |
| **Apr 2025** | 68.32 |
| **Mar 2025** | 62.62 |
| **Feb 2025** | 70.32 |
| **Jan 2025** | 69.64 |
| **Dec 2024** | 99.97 |
| **Nov 2024** | 81.51 |
| **Oct 2024** | 56.8 |
| **Sep 2024** | 62.9 |

**Source:** [Statista](https://www.statista.com/statistics/1108992/fortnite-number-viewers/)

Fortnite’s popularity on Twitch also ties into the platform’s massive audience growth. Check out the [latest Twitch user statistics](https://www.demandsage.com/twitch-users/) to see how Fortnite compares within the wider streaming ecosystem.

### **Fortnite On Discord**

Fortnite has one of the biggest servers on Discord in the gaming community, with over 1.23 million members.

You can [join the channel from here](https://discord.com/invite/fortnite).

![Fortnite On Discord
](https://www.demandsage.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Fortnite-On-Discord.jpeg "Fortnite On Discord")

### **Conclusion: Fortnite Has over 1.3 Million Daily Active Users**

Fortnite keeps a loyal following through its broad accessibility across different platforms and its massive esports prize pools, which have exceeded $79 million.

With players dedicating an average of 6–10 hours a week and spending over $102 annually on in-game purchases, the game has secured its place as a social hub.

As it continues to integrate new technologies like VR and AI, Fortnite’s role as both a game and a digital destination is firmly secured.

## **About Fortnite**

![Fortnite Live Player Count](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20577'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Fortnite is an online video game that has three unique modes of play. It was released in 2017 by **[EpicGames](https://www.epicgames.com)**. Although the general gameplay is the same yet, you find quite a lot of difference while playing each of the three separate mods.

In the first mode, the game is known as Fortnite: Save the World. Here, up to four players in this game collaborate to defend their home base or shelters.

The game starts after a devastating storm wipes out 98% of the population. After that, the survivors have a tough time beating out zombies that attack them. Meanwhile, players also need to accumulate resources, and these are like hero characters, weapons, and traps.

![Fortnite Map](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20577'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

The other gameplay mode is the Fortnite Battle Royale and is a player versus player game. Some hundred players are allowed in this game and even allow a player to play alone, in duo, or squad the latter three or four players. Here, in this game, players that have no weapons initially are air-dropped on the map.

The moment they land they must defend themselves and scout the whole area for weapons, resources, vehicles, and more. Whatever they may do their main objective is to remain alive.

The third mode of the game is known as Fortnite Creative and is essentially a sandbox game. Players have complete freedom to spawn anything on an island where the game begins. It is a highly creative game and full of challenges.

In all three modes, the player’s main objective is to remain alive as long as possible.

Fornite is a popular game we couldn’t miss creating a live player count for it. So, if you want to know [how many people play fortnite](https://activeplayer.io/fornite/) simply follow this page to be updated.

## **Fornite Fact**

Fortnite game is an entirely online game and this means you cannot play it offline. It is also a non-steam game and is a shooter survival game all through. In Fortnite Creative, essentially sandbox game players may use pick-ax to breakdown structures on the map to build other structures.
